summary:
The removal of weak enamel rods strengthens the tooth structure, while ensuring lap-slapping fit provides proper marginal adaptation; placing the margin in self-cleanable areas promotes hygiene without compromising restorations' longevity. Increased metal burnishability pertains more to fabrication processes outside direct clinical preparations involving gingival margins. Thus, it does not align with typical requirements during class II inlay gingival margin preparation procedures focused purely clinically. ```plaintext
Key steps like removing weak enamel rods and creating a lap-slapping fit ensure structural strength and precise placement, whereas increased metal burnishability relates to production methods unrelated to immediate clinical preparation goals.
```
